questions.text: Work done is zero when the angle between force and displacement is:
questions.explanation: W = F d cos θ. cos 90° = 0.

questions.text: The Work-Energy Theorem states that work done by all forces equals:
questions.explanation: W_net = ΔK = K_final - K_initial.

questions.text: A 1 kg mass has a kinetic energy of 1 Joule. Its speed is:
questions.explanation: K = ½mv² => 1 = ½(1)v² => v² = 2 => v = sqrt(2).

questions.text: Which of the following is a non-conservative force?
questions.explanation: Work done by friction depends on the path taken.

questions.text: The rate of doing work is called:
questions.explanation: P = W/t.

questions.text: Potential energy of a spring of constant 'k' stretched by 'x' is:
questions.explanation: Elastic potential energy formula.

questions.text: In an elastic collision, which are conserved?
questions.explanation: Total momentum and total kinetic energy are both conserved in perfectly elastic collisions.

questions.text: In a perfectly inelastic collision, the two bodies:
questions.explanation: They move with a common velocity after impact.

questions.text: The SI unit of power is:
questions.explanation: 1 Watt = 1 Joule per second.

questions.text: Kilowatt-hour (kWh) is a unit of:
questions.explanation: It is a commercial unit of energy (Power x Time).

questions.text: If momentum is doubled, kinetic energy becomes:
questions.explanation: K = p²/2m. If p -> 2p, K -> 4p²/2m = 4K.

questions.text: Mechanical energy is the sum of:
questions.explanation: E = K + V.

questions.text: A dot product of force and velocity gives:
questions.explanation: P = F·v.

questions.text: Work done by a centripetal force is:
questions.explanation: Centripetal force is always perpendicular to displacement in circular motion.

questions.text: Coefficient of restitution (e) for a perfectly elastic collision is:
questions.explanation: e = (v2-v1)/(u1-u2) = 1 for elastic.

questions.text: Gravitational potential energy of a body of mass 'm' at height 'h' is:
questions.explanation: Energy due to position in a gravitational field.

questions.text: When a spring is compressed, the work done by the external force is:
questions.explanation: Force and displacement are in the same direction.

questions.text: One horsepower is equal to:
questions.explanation: Standard conversion used in engineering.

questions.text: Work done by a conservative force over a closed path is:
questions.explanation: Definition of a conservative force.

questions.text: Energy can neither be created nor destroyed. this is the Law of Conservation of:
questions.explanation: First law of thermodynamics / General energy principle.

questions.text: The slope of a Potential Energy vs Position (U-x) graph gives:
questions.explanation: F = -dU/dx.

questions.text: A light and a heavy body have equal kinetic energy. Which one has more momentum?
questions.explanation: p = sqrt(2mK). For same K, p ∝ sqrt(m).

questions.text: Which form of energy is possessed by a flying bird?
questions.explanation: Kinetic due to motion and potential due to height.

questions.text: Work done by friction is usually:
questions.explanation: Friction usually opposes displacement.

questions.text: Power is a _____ quantity.
questions.explanation: It is the dot product of F and v, which results in a scalar.

questions.text: The energy stored in a stretched rubber band is:
questions.explanation: Specifically elastic potential energy.

questions.text: When a body falls freely, its total mechanical energy:
questions.explanation: Potential energy converts to kinetic energy, but sum remains constant.

questions.text: In which collision is only momentum conserved?
questions.explanation: In inelastic collisions, some kinetic energy is lost as heat/deformation.

questions.text: The area under a Force-Displacement (F-d) graph represents:
questions.explanation: Integral of F·dx is work.

questions.text: What is the work done in holding a 50kg suitcase for 10 minutes at a fixed spot?
questions.explanation: Displacement is zero, so work is zero.

questions.text: The dimension of work is:
questions.explanation: Force [MLT⁻²] * Distance [L] = [ML²T⁻²].

questions.text: The dot product of two vectors is commutative, meaning:
questions.explanation: Scalar multiplication is independent of order.

questions.text: Collision of gas molecules is considered to be:
questions.explanation: An assumption in the Kinetic Theory of Gases.

questions.text: If a body is moving in a circle, the work done by the tension in the string is:
questions.explanation: Tension is perpendicular to displacement.

questions.text: Elastic Potential Energy is always:
questions.explanation: ½kx² is always positive for any displacement x.

questions.text: Energy possessed by a body due to its motion is:
questions.explanation: Basic definition of kinetic energy.

questions.text: The power of a pump which can lift 100kg of water to 10m in 10s is (g=10):
Work done is a _____ quantity.
questions.explanation: It has no direction.

questions.text: 1 Joule is equal to:
questions.explanation: Conversion between SI and CGS units of work.

questions.text: If the kinetic energy of a body is increased by 300%, its momentum increases by:
questions.explanation: K2 = 4K1. p ∝ sqrt(K), so p2 = sqrt(4)p1 = 2p1. Increase is 100%.
